paper_metadata.title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows
paper_metadata.abstract: This paper presents a new vision Transformer, called Swin Transformer, that capably serves as a general-purpose backbone for computer vision. Challenges in adapting Transformer from language to vision arise from differences between the two domains, such as large variations in the scale of visual entities and the high resolution of pixels in images compared to words in text. To address these differences, we propose a hierarchical Transformer whose representation is computed with \textbf{S}hifted \textbf{win}dows. The shifted windowing scheme brings greater efficiency by limiting self-attention computation to non-overlapping local windows while also allowing for cross-window connection. This hierarchical architecture has the flexibility to model at various scales and has linear computational complexity with respect to image size. These qualities of Swin Transformer make it compatible with a broad range of vision tasks, including image classification (87.3 top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K) and dense prediction tasks such as object detection (58.7 box AP and 51.1 mask AP on COCO test-dev) and semantic segmentation (53.5 mIoU on ADE20K val). Its performance surpasses the previous state-of-the-art by a large margin of +2.7 box AP and +2.6 mask AP on COCO, and +3.2 mIoU on ADE20K, demonstrating the potential of Transformer-based models as vision backbones. The hierarchical design and the shifted window approach also prove beneficial for all-MLP architectures. The code and models are publicly available at~\url{https://github.com/microsoft/Swin-Transformer}.

extraction_data.innovation: Swin Transformer introduces shifted window-based self-attention to enable efficient and scalable self-attention in vision tasks, reducing quadratic complexity to linear with respect to image resolution.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (not specified in paper; inferred from context)
extraction_data.extension: Shifted window partitioning enables efficient self-attention computation by reducing quadratic complexity to linear, enabling large-scale vision models.
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient and accurate visual modeling with linear complexity
extraction_data.application: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.paper_title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows

extraction_data.innovation: Swin Transformer introduces a hierarchical feature representation using shifted windows in self-attention, enabling linear computational complexity and improved efficiency over sliding window methods.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (not specified in paper; inferred from context)
extraction_data.extension: Swin-T reduces model size and computational complexity by 0.25× compared to Swin-B while retaining performance.
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient and accurate visual modeling with linear complexity
extraction_data.application: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.paper_title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows

extraction_data.innovation: Swin-L is a large variant of Swin-B with 2× computational complexity and similar performance.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text corpus (not specified in paper; inferred from context)
extraction_data.application: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient and scalable self-attention for vision tasks
extraction_data.extension: Swin-L increases model size and computational complexity by 2× compared to Swin-B while maintaining performance.
extraction_data.paper_title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows

extraction_data.innovation: Swin Transformer introduces a hierarchical Transformer architecture with shifted windows for efficient and effective feature learning, enabling better performance with fewer parameters compared to previous models.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient and effective vision models for image recognition and segmentation
extraction_data.paper_title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows

extraction_data.innovation: Swin-B extends the Swin architecture with more parameters for improved performance on ImageNet-1K fine-tuning, achieving 83.3% top-1 accuracy with 88M parameters.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.research_problem: Large-scale vision models for image recognition and segmentation
extraction_data.extension: The model uses an improved training framework (HTC++) which enhances performance by 0.4% over standard training, demonstrating the impact of training optimization on model capabilities.
extraction_data.paper_title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows

extraction_data.innovation: Swin-L is a larger variant of Swin with 197M parameters, achieving 87.3% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K fine-tuning, demonstrating the scalability of the Swin architecture.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.research_problem: Large-scale vision models for image recognition and segmentation
extraction_data.paper_title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows

extraction_data.innovation: Swin-L with HTC++ achieves 57.1% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K, showing the effectiveness of the Swin architecture with advanced training methods.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, object detection, semantic segmentation
extraction_data.research_problem: Large-scale vision models for image recognition and segmentation
extraction_data.extension: The model benefits from an improved training framework (HTC++) which enhances performance by 0.7% over standard training, highlighting the importance of training optimization in achieving state-of-the-art results.
extraction_data.paper_title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows

extraction_data.innovation: Swin Transformer introduces a hierarchical architecture with shifted window-based self-attention, enabling efficient and effective feature learning for vision tasks. It uses a decoder-based architecture with multi-stage self-attention and stochastic depth augmentation.
extraction_data.application: Image classification, semantic segmentation, object detection
extraction_data.research_problem: Efficient and effective vision models for image classification and segmentation
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised discriminative fine-tuning (e.g., ImageNet-1K classification, COCO object detection, ADE20K segmentation)
extraction_data.extension: Shifted window-based self-attention for efficient hierarchical feature learning
extraction_data.paper_title: Swin Transformer: Hierarchical Vision Transformer using Shifted Windows

extraction_data.innovation: Swin Transformer introduces a hierarchical architecture with shifted window-based self-attention, enabling efficient and effective feature learning for vision tasks. It uses a decoder-based architecture with multi-stage self-attention and stochastic depth augmentation.
